description.en:
Moisture System
Transform your farming experience with a sophisticated moisture simulation system that brings realism and strategic depth to Farming Simulator 25. This comprehensive mod introduces dynamic moisture mechanics that fundamentally change how you approach crop management, harvest timing, and storage decisions.
Experience authentic environmental interactions where moisture levels fluctuate naturally across your farm based on terrain elevation, prevailing weather patterns (rainfall, snowfall, temperature fluctuations), and day/night cycles. Fields at lower elevations stay wetter while higher ground dries faster, so consider moisture levels when planning which crops to plant where and when to harvest. Every farming decision gains new weight as you balance optimal timing against changing conditions.
Advanced Crop Quality System: Your harvested yields are no longer uniform commodities. Each load receives a quality grade from A (premium) through D (poor) based on its moisture content at harvest, with direct economic consequences at the point of sale. Every crop species has been carefully calibrated with unique optimal moisture windows that reflect real-world agricultural standards. Master these ranges and time your operations precisely to maximize profitability. Access comprehensive grade tables and seasonal moisture projections through the integrated in-game interface (Shift+M).
Features: - Terrain-aware field moisture simulation with monthly variation patterns across three distinct environment profiles (Dry/Wet/Normal) - Sophisticated grass management system featuring progressive tedding mechanics, weather-responsive drying rates, and automatic hay conversion at optimal moisture thresholds. Multiple tedding passes accelerates drying if you need it. - Ground rot: Grass and straw piles left exposed accumulate rain exposure over time, triggering progressive volume loss as organic material deteriorates. Timing your collection and baling operations becomes critical. Hay will revert to grass if it gets too wet. - Bale rotting: Unprotected bales track cumulative rain exposure with initial tolerance periods, escalating decay rates based on exposure history, and limited recovery during dry spells. Strategic use of wrapping and shelter infrastructure protects your investment. - Purchase the moisture meter hand tool in the store for precise field and pile readings on demand (You can display this in the HUD if preferred).
Extensive customization options allow you to calibrate moisture dynamics, tedding efficiency, decay parameters, and interface preferences to match your preferred gameplay experience. Whether you seek hardcore realism or a gentler introduction to moisture management, the system adapts to your vision.
Github: https://github.com/sprkem/FS25_MoistureSystem
Beta Changelog:
0.0.0.9: - Multiplayer initial client state event added. Lots of other multiplayer fixes. - Validate ground piles on save to ensure we don't save empty cells - Fix active cleanup of piles during baling and improved moisture aggregation - Improve stored bale save logic to only track bales with moisture/rot data
0.0.0.8: - Ensure ground material rot is affected by the game timescale. This replaces previous rates so please give feedback! - Save moisture to 1 dp to reduce save file size growth. - Add a setting to let you see HUD field moisture without using the hand tool. Default is off. - Add a setting to control how the moisture meter displays readings (blinking alert vs notification). Default is blinking alert. - Only the top range will show the % in the crop grade menu, to reduce clutter. - Ensure pile properties are merged and cleaned when using a windrower. - Made object moisture updates multiplayer compatible. - Add bale rotting multiplayer sync event. - Improve meter positioning in third person. - Rewrote pile saving format to be resilient to fill type indices changing and reduce file size. The previous format should load correctly still.
0.0.0.7: - Add a moisture meter hand tool for measuring ground moisture levels in the field. You can no longer see ground moisture in the HUD. - Rotting bales in storage will continue to rot. - Reworked ground material rotting to be based on rain exposure.
0.0.0.6: - Wrapped grass bales will track moisture % on the bale. When the bale is done fermenting, volume will be reduced based on moisture thresholds. - Straw that is baled will track moisture on the baler. The output bale will have rain exposure % applied if over certain thresholds. - Hay will revert to grass if moisture goes about he 7% threshold. - Include straw bales in the bale rotting system. - Add missing setting strings.
0.0.0.5: - Grass and straw on the ground over 15% moisture will rot away - Fix logic issue where grass pile moisture wasn't increasing in the rain
0.0.0.4: - Fix game breaking deposting material into husbandries. - Adds bale rotting system.
0.0.0.3: - Fix an issue when harvesting different moisture levels and how volume weighting is calculated. - Fix issues with forage wagons keeping moisture levels when unloading and loading. - Reduce the a delay for processing cells, the goal being that hay conversion happens closer to the implement. - When grass is tedded during a contract, base game behavior applies.
0.0.0.2: - Add console command "msSetMoisture" for testing (usage: msSetMoisture value[1-100]). Will not violate the months range. - Delay application of tedding moisture reduction briefly for better rendering - Address an issue where tedding adjacent up/down wide spread rows would apply tedding reduction to middle sections twice. - Reduces the cell size for more fidelity. Revised how the data is saved to keep file size small. This will need monitoring as people play. - Add missing hail weather detection and impact on moisture levels.
0.0.0.1: - Initial release
